commit.message:
perf: batched sensitivity analysis with jax.vmap (#153)

Add vmap-based batched energy evaluation to JAX engines so that
compute_sensitivity() can evaluate all 2N+1 parameter perturbations
in a single vectorized call instead of sequentially.

Engine layer:
- MMEngine base: supports_batched_energy() / batched_energy() with
  sequential fallback for non-JAX backends
- JaxEngine: vmap over _energy_fn with cached JIT compilation
- JaxMDEngine: vmap over _scalar_energy_fn, broadcasting coords
  and neighbor list

Objective layer:
- is_energy_only(): detect pure-energy reference sets
- batched_scores(): dispatch to batched or sequential path
- _batched_energy_scores(): vectorized residual computation

Cycling layer:
- compute_sensitivity() builds (2N+1, P) param matrix upfront
  and calls batched_scores() for the entire sensitivity sweep

Tests:
- 5 new JAX-specific tests covering batched_energy, is_energy_only,
  batched_scores consistency, and end-to-end compute_sensitivity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: align license to MIT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add OWL ontology tutorial page with embedded live demos

New files:
- packages/web/public/owl-tutorial.html
  Full step-by-step tutorial: start from template → add ontology
  header → place classes → build hierarchy → add instances →
  add object properties → run auto-layout → export to Turtle.
  Each section has prose instructions and an embedded live BoxesEditor
  iframe pre-loaded with that stage of the Animals ontology.
  Includes a highlighted Turtle code block for the export step,
  a sticky TOC with IntersectionObserver active-section tracking,
  and a responsive dark-theme layout.

- packages/web/public/demo.html + demo.js
  Thin iframe-friendly BoxesEditor wrapper.
  Params: ?graph=<url> to load a .boxes file, ?tab=<id> to activate
  a sidebar tab, ?sidebar=0 to hide the sidebar for canvas-only views.

- packages/web/public/demos/owl-{1..7}-*.boxes
  Seven incremental demo graphs for the tutorial iframes, each a
  valid .boxes file (OWL palette + stylesheet + Animals ontology
  elements at preset positions):
    1-empty      — empty canvas, palette visible
    2-ontology   — owl:Ontology header node
    3-classes    — 7 owl:Class nodes, no edges yet
    4-hierarchy  — subClassOf edges connected
    5-instances  — individuals typed via rdf:type
    6-properties — owl:ObjectProperty + Person class
    7-complete   — full graph ready for layout

Modified:
- packages/core/src/boxes-editor.js
  Add public setActiveTab(tabId) method so demo.js can activate
  a specific sidebar tab programmatically.

- packages/web/public/index.html
  Add 'Building an OWL Ontology' tutorial link on welcome screen.

- packages/web/vite.config.js
  Add demo.html + owl-tutorial.html as Rollup entry points.
  Add copyDemoFiles() plugin to copy demos/*.boxes → dist/demos/
  during production build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add OpenRouter as primary provider with task-specific model routing

Configure OpenRouter as the primary LLM provider with intelligent
model selection per task type:

- code_generation → Qwen3 Coder (purpose-built for code, 262K ctx)
- planning → DeepSeek Chat V3.1 (strong reasoning)
- analysis → Gemini 2.5 Flash (1M ctx for large codebases)
- critique/quality → DeepSeek R1 (deep reasoning)
- fast/ingest/verification → Gemini 2.0 Flash (cheapest capable)
- fallback → Llama 3.3 70B (free, rate-limit safety net)

Changes:
- settings.json: primary_provider=openrouter, 11 task routes
- model_adapter.py: OpenRouter first in respond() fallback chain,
  respond_for_role() routes to task-specific models via OpenRouter,
  call_openrouter() uses model_routing config with auto-fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Expand guided tour from 9 to 14 steps

New steps cover features not previously in the tour:
- Undo & Redo (↩/↪ buttons and Ctrl+Z/Y shortcuts)
- Stylesheet tab — editing Cytoscape.js CSS rules inline
- Palette editing — adding/editing/deleting node and edge types
- Import & Export — RDF/Turtle, JSON-LD, RDF/XML menus
- Templates & Reuse — .boxes files as reusable templates, Save As workflow

Updated existing steps:
- Welcome is now generic (not OWL-specific)
- Canvas step adds Fit (F), Move, and trackpad pinch-zoom
- Palette step clarifies Esc to deactivate
- Properties step notes live updates
- Layout step adds more algorithms (Concentric, Grid, Circle, Random)
- Save step expanded to cover Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S), Open, and New
- Final step uses generic graph workflow instead of OWL-specific

Bumped TOUR_DONE_KEY to 'boxes-tour-v2-done' so returning users
who completed v1 see the expanded tour automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: schema-driven ForceField param-vector methods (#173)

- Add _PARAM_SLOTS class variable as single source of truth for
  parameter vector layout (collection, slot names)
- Rewrite n_params, get_param_vector, set_param_vector, with_params
  to iterate _PARAM_SLOTS instead of hardcoded per-collection loops
- Reduces ~40 lines of duplicated iteration to ~15 lines of
  schema-driven logic

Partially addresses #173

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract shared _params_and_coords to _jax_common (#172)

- Add params_and_coords() to _jax_common.py as shared helper
- JaxEngine and JaxMDEngine delegate to shared implementation
- Energy kernels left as-is (fundamentally different: PBC vs non-PBC)

Partially addresses #172

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: deduplicate evaluator extraction and registry dispatch (#171)

- Add HANDLED_KINDS to all evaluators for uniform kind registration
- GeometryEvaluator.extract_value() now delegates to _extract() via
  temporary GeometryResult (removes 40 lines of duplicated logic)
- EigenmatrixEvaluator.extract_value() delegates to _extract() via
  temporary EigenmatrixResult (removes 12 lines of duplicated logic)
- ObjectiveFunction uses _kind_to_evaluator registry dict built from
  evaluator HANDLED_KINDS (replaces hardcoded if/elif dispatch)
- ObjectiveFunction.reset() iterates evaluators generically

Closes #171

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add skills list and Add Skill button to roles index card

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract shared engine infrastructure to MMEngine base (#170)

- Add coerce_molecule() to base.py for duck-typed molecule coercion
  (handles Q2MMMolecule, engine handles with .molecule, str/Path)
- Promote frequencies() from abstract to default implementation in
  MMEngine (hessian → hessian_to_frequencies pipeline)
- Remove duplicated frequencies() from JaxEngine and JaxMDEngine
- Remove duplicated _as_molecule() from both JAX engines, replaced
  with coerce_molecule() calls
- OpenMM and Tinker retain their overrides (different input patterns)

Closes #170

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use top-level constant references in Transcript::Analysable

Ruby resolves constants relative to the enclosing module, so OpenAI,
Faraday, and JSON were being looked up as Transcript::Analysable::*
rather than at the top level. Prefix all three with :: to fix.

refactor: consolidate geometry math and magic constants (#169)

- Create q2mm/geometry.py as canonical source for bond_length(),
  bond_angle(), and dihedral_angle() calculations
- Delegate all existing implementations to the new module
- Add DEFAULT_BOND_TOLERANCE (1.3) and REAL_FREQUENCY_THRESHOLD (50.0)
  to constants.py
- Update tinker.py, objective.py, reference_yaml.py, and benchmark.py
  to use centralized constants
- Add np.clip guard in bond_angle() to prevent arccos domain errors

Closes #169

recharts v3.3.0 (PR #6388) introduced the `responsive` prop built-in
to all charts, making ResponsiveContainer redundant. Replace wrapper
with direct props on LineChart and AreaChart.

repository.name: stocksense
repository.description: StockSense is an AI-powered stock prediction platform built with Flask. It uses a local Ollama LLM (phi-mini model) combined with ensemble ML models to generate stock forecasts, with real-time WebSocket updates and automated price-movement alerts.

commit.message:
fix: guard Atom.element against invalid atomic_num and reorder is_dummy

- Atom.element now raises ValueError for None/0/negative atomic_num
  instead of silently returning wrong element via negative indexing
- Atom.is_dummy checks atom_type_name and atomic_num before attempting
  element derivation, preventing crashes on dummy atoms (atomic_num=-2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix safe.directory for container-based test jobs

actions/checkout@v4 sets safe.directory using a temporary HOME
override that doesn't persist for subsequent steps. When
vcs-versioning runs git during `pip install -e .`, it uses the
real HOME (/root) where the config doesn't exist, causing
"dubious ownership" failures.

Add explicit `git config --global --add safe.directory` before
pip install in all 6 container-based jobs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add palette editing UI and public palette mutation API

Palette items now show edit (✎) and delete (×) buttons on hover.
'+ Add node type' and '+ Add edge type' buttons appear below each section.
Clicking edit or add opens an inline form with:
  - Node types: label, id, background color, border color, shape
  - Edge types: label, id, color, line style
  - Both: optional JSON data template for default node/edge properties

Public API additions on BoxesEditor:
  addNodeType(type), updateNodeType(id, updates), removeNodeType(id)
  addEdgeType(type), updateEdgeType(id, updates), removeEdgeType(id)
All mutations re-render the palette and emit a 'paletteChanged' event.

Together with the palette-in-file-format change, this lets users build and
save custom palettes directly from the editor UI, making it easy to create
new templates by designing a palette and saving the file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address PR review — docstring, comment, and latent bug fixes

- Remove stale Hessian reference from module docstring
- Remove incorrect inline comment on atomic_num
- Fix DOF.as_data() UnboundLocalError for unrecognized subclasses
- Fix Structure.coords and format_coords using self._atoms directly
  instead of self.atoms property (would crash if atoms not yet set)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

task/kmz-csv-and-contour-map-endpoints-v1-rest-api-jaia-2211 (#1514)

* Move JDV's kmz module for backend kmz file generation into pyjaia

* Added KMZRequest type to REST v1
Added a kmz field to ask for a kmz file containing task packets in a time range

* Added kmz endpoint to REST API v1

* Added the kmz endpoint to the APIConfig.APIKey data structures, so it can be used when an API key is required and provided.

* Use environment variables to configure the REST API for the kmz_test.py script, and default to not dumping the kmz file.
This makes the file work better as a no-side-effect pytest script.

* Update src/web/rest_api/common/shared_data.py

Pad by +/- 1 second, to account for DCCL rounding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use APIResponse, instead of the verbose namespaced version.

* jaia_api_long can also return non-APIResponse, for things like kmz and csv downloads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix query_task_packets docstring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make minor adjustments to docstrings

* Move the jaialog_store module from jdv into pyjaia.  This allows us to use the JaialogStore class in REST API v1.

* Remove double-checking of isinstance(response, APIResponse)

* We should parse the TaskPacket json strings leniently, to account for packets JSON-encoded with newer protobuf field names.

* Merge kmz and task_packet queries into one endpoint with a format parameter.

* Add csv file generation to REST API v1

* Added CONTOUR_GEOJSON format to the task_packets endpoint.

* Copilot suggestions:
* bool is a subclass of int, so check for bool before checking for int
* Allow long form API requests to return non-APIResponse bodies
* Use list comprehension, rather than subtraction operator to remove first_keys
* Use f-string in warning message
* Remove unused imports
* Use a mask when printing the API key for privacy
* Remove the unused get_kmz function, for separation of concerns (TaskPacketDatabase should only handle querying of task packets from the db)

* Copilot suggestions:
* Fix the generation of sql set placeholders from lists of strings, i.e. "(?, ?, ?)"
* Add test to cover the multibot query case
* Add defaults to LogDescription, LogDirectory, and FileDownload
* Remove unused MissionTask import
* Remove the http status code 400 for errors (the REST API v1 tests expect 200 even for errors)
* Remove wildcard import in csv kmz test
* Rename `format` variable to `output_format` to avoid shadowing built-in function

* Use all=True in task packet query tests (except for the multibot querying test)

* Test single and multibot querying in the taskpacket query pytest.

* Update docs

* Make comments more concise

* Remvoe duplicate file

* Import .objects into namespace

* Add some comments and a docstring.

---------

commit.message:
refactor: Move structure classes from parsers to models

Move Atom, DOF, Bond, Angle, Torsion, and Structure from the
deprecated q2mm.parsers.structures module to q2mm.models.structure.

These are general-purpose data classes, not parser-specific code.
The old module is now a thin deprecation shim that re-exports
from the new location and emits a DeprecationWarning.

All 6 consuming parsers and the models/molecule.py TYPE_CHECKING
import updated to use the canonical q2mm.models.structure path.
Lazy imports used in DOF.as_data() and Structure.identify_angles()
to avoid a circular import through parsers/__init__.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract shared JAX utilities into _jax_common.py

jax_engine.py and jax_md_engine.py duplicated JAX import guards,
float64 config, _ensure_jax(), parameter-vector offset calculations,
and ForceField matching helpers (_match_bond, _match_angle, _match_vdw).
Extract shared code into _jax_common.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GeoJSON oneOf benchmark infrastructure

Add benchmark comparing V5 baseline (CLI-generated frozen code) vs V5 current
(source-generated with discriminator optimization) for GeoJSON schema validation.

The GeoJSON schema has 9 oneOf branches with a 'type' discriminator property.
Benchmark shows ~15% improvement (ratio 0.85) with the discriminator optimization.

- GeoJsonBenchmarkModels project with CLI baseline (B/) and source-gen entry (V5/)
- geojson-schema.json with 9 geometry type branches
- geojson-instances.jsonl with 500 real-world GeoJSON instances
- BenchmarkGeoJsonOneOfValidation benchmark class

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: deduplicate TinkerFF and TinkerMM3A via _TinkerBase

Extract shared __init__, lines property, and import_ff into _TinkerBase
base class. TinkerFF and TinkerMM3A now only define their distinct
export_ff methods. Promote keyword lists to module constants.

486 → 328 lines (33% reduction), identical behavior preserved.

refactor: extract shared hessian_to_frequencies utility

All 4 MM engines independently implemented the same Hessian →
mass-weight → diagonalize → cm⁻¹ pipeline. Extract into
hessian_to_frequencies() in q2mm/models/hessian.py and update
OpenMM, JAX, JAX-MD, and Tinker backends to use it.

commit.message:
feat: build complete interview evaluation web application

- app.py: animated landing page with gradient hero, workflow steps, feature cards, CTA
- pages/1_Auth.py: login/register/forgot-password with two-column layout
- pages/2_Dashboard.py: stats cards, quick-access nav, recent evaluations table
- pages/3_Projects.py: full CRUD with tech-stack chips, cascading delete confirmation
- pages/4_Roles.py: full CRUD with project linking, question-count confirmation
- pages/5_Questions.py: full CRUD with category/difficulty/role filters
- pages/6_Evaluate_Candidate.py: 4-step flow – setup, AI analysis, questions, submit
- pages/7_Archives.py: search/filter, inline status update, PDF download, delete
- utils/database.py: SQLAlchemy models (users/projects/roles/questions/evaluations/password_resets) + CRUD helpers
- utils/auth.py: bcrypt password hashing/verification, session-state auth helpers
- utils/email_utils.py: SMTP reset-code email with Gmail/Outlook/Yahoo support
- utils/ai_utils.py: LangChain + GPT-4o-mini resume analysis and question generation
- utils/pdf_utils.py: ReportLab professional evaluation PDF generator
- .streamlit/config.toml: custom Indigo theme + 10 MB upload limit
- requirements.txt: all pinned dependencies
- .env.example: documented environment variables

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt
